Matern Workflow

Overview

ebnm_Matern_generator() builds an ebnm-compatible smoother using a stationary Matern field represented through an INLA/SPDE mesh. The input locations may be one-dimensional or two-dimensional.

This vignette focuses on the known-standard-error generator workflow. If s is unknown and you want to learn one common noise SD instead, use eb_smoother(). The high-level API supports family = "matern", link = "log", s = NULL, so it can compare a non-negative smooth Matern prior against family = "constant", link = "log", which is the corresponding non-negative constant-baseline prior.

The identity link uses the exact Gaussian backend by default. The log link is available for positive mean functions and uses the Fisher Laplace backend by default under backend = "auto". Explicit backend = "laplace" keeps the older observed-Hessian Laplace semantics.

There are multiple Matern backends:

  • For link = "identity", backend = "auto" uses the exact Gaussian EB backend. If pc.penalty is supplied, the PC prior contributes to the hyperparameter objective.
  • If link = "log", backend = "auto" resolves to backend = "laplace_fisher". Fisher fits keep the same conditional mode as the log-link posterior objective, but use Fisher/Gauss-Newton curvature for the returned posterior approximation and score.
  • Explicit backend = "laplace" uses observed-Hessian Laplace semantics. backend = "laplace_tmb" remains a compatibility alias for this route.
  • Explicit backend = "inla" fits use INLA. The compatibility alias backend = "inla_pc" is accepted when pc.penalty is supplied, but the returned fit reports the canonical backend label backend = "inla". For log-link fits, the primary comparable log_likelihood is the package observed-Hessian Laplace objective evaluated at the INLA mode.

One-Dimensional Example

set.seed(42)

locations_1d <- seq(0, 12, length.out = 120)
truth_1d <- exp(sin(locations_1d / 1.5) + 0.2 * cos(locations_1d))
s_1d <- sample(c(0.15, 0.25, 0.35), length(locations_1d), replace = TRUE)
x_1d <- truth_1d + rnorm(length(locations_1d), sd = s_1d)

fit_fun_1d <- ebnm_Matern_generator(
  locations = locations_1d,
  link = "log"
)

fit_1d <- fit_fun_1d(x_1d, s_1d)

head(fit_1d$posterior)
#>       mean         var second_moment
#> 1 1.305783 0.006020813      1.711091
#> 2 1.315072 0.005395279      1.734809
#> 3 1.343464 0.004651977      1.809547
#> 4 1.392628 0.004422518      1.943836
#> 5 1.462127 0.004733118      2.142549
#> 6 1.549903 0.005291867      2.407491
fit_1d$fitted_g
#> $theta
#> [1] 1.791909
#>
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.7416781
#>
#> $beta
#> [1] 0.08983087
#>
#> $beta_prec
#> NULL
#>
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Matern"
fit_1d$fitted_beta
#> [1] 0.08983087
fit_1d$backend
#> [1] "laplace_fisher"
plot(locations_1d, x_1d, pch = 16, cex = 0.45, col = "grey55",
     xlab = "location", ylab = "x",
     main = "Matern smoother in one dimension")
lines(locations_1d, truth_1d, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_1d$posterior$mean, col = "navy", lwd = 2)

PC-Prior Fits

Supplying pc.penalty lets the Matern fitter select smoothing hyperparameters under a PC prior. With link = "identity" and backend = "auto", this remains on the package exact Gaussian backend. Use explicit backend = "inla" when an independent INLA/SPDE comparison is needed.

fit_fun_pc <- ebnm_Matern_generator(
  locations = locations_1d,
  link = "identity",
  pc.penalty = list(
    range = c(1.2, 0.5),
    sigma = c(0.6, 0.5)
  )
)

fit_pc <- fit_fun_pc(x_1d, s_1d)
pc_breakdown <- matern_objective_breakdown(fit_pc)

fit_pc$backend
#> [1] "exact"
fit_pc$fitted_g
#> $theta
#> [1] 1.633616
#>
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.8534111
#>
#> $beta
#> [1] 1.365165
#>
#> $beta_prec
#> NULL
#>
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Matern"
fit_pc$fitted_beta
#> [1] 1.365165
fit_pc$log_likelihood
#> 'log Lik.' -20.85636 (df=NULL)
fit_pc$log_likelihood_stepA_mlik_integration
#> NULL
pc_breakdown$objective_integrated_flat_plus_prior
#> NULL
pc_breakdown$recorded_minus_matched_exact
#> [1] 0
plot(locations_1d, x_1d, pch = 16, cex = 0.45, col = "grey55",
     xlab = "location", ylab = "x",
     main = "Fisher Laplace and exact PC-prior Matern fits")
lines(locations_1d, truth_1d, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 1)
lines(locations_1d, fit_1d$posterior$mean, col = "navy", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_pc$posterior$mean, col = "darkorange3", lwd = 2, lty = 3)
legend(
  "topright",
  legend = c("truth", "Fisher Laplace", "exact PC prior"),
  col = c("firebrick", "navy", "darkorange3"),
  lty = c(1, 2, 3),
  lwd = c(2, 2, 2),
  bty = "n"
)

Fixed Hyperparameters and Fixed Intercept

When fix_g = TRUE, the Matern smoother fixes the smoothing hyperparameters from g_init. Supply beta_fixed separately when the intercept should also be fixed.

set.seed(99)

fit_fixed <- fit_fun_1d(
  x_1d,
  s_1d,
  g_init = Matern(theta = log(1.2), sigma = 0.7),
  fix_g = TRUE,
  beta_fixed = 0.1
)

fit_fixed$fitted_g
#> $theta
#> [1] 0.1823216
#>
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.7
#>
#> $beta
#> [1] 0.1
#>
#> $beta_prec
#> NULL
#>
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "Matern"
fit_fixed$fitted_beta
#> [1] 0.1
plot(locations_1d, x_1d, pch = 16, cex = 0.45, col = "grey55",
     xlab = "location", ylab = "x",
     main = "Matern smoother with fixed hyperparameters")
lines(locations_1d, truth_1d, col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
lines(locations_1d, fit_fixed$posterior$mean, col = "darkgreen", lwd = 2)

Range/sigma fixing is not supported by the INLA backend. If pc.penalty is supplied and you need fixed Matern hyperparameters, fit an unpenalized package backend for that fixed-parameter score instead of requesting an INLA PC-prior fit.

Two-Dimensional Example

For a two-dimensional domain, pass an n x 2 matrix of coordinates.

set.seed(2026)

nx <- 14
ny <- 10
grid <- expand.grid(
  x = seq(0, 1, length.out = nx),
  y = seq(0, 1, length.out = ny)
)
locations_2d <- as.matrix(grid)

truth_2d <- with(
  grid,
  sin(2 * pi * x) * cos(2 * pi * y)
)
s_2d <- rep(0.15, nrow(locations_2d))
x_2d <- truth_2d + rnorm(nrow(locations_2d), sd = s_2d)

fit_fun_2d <- ebnm_Matern_generator(
  locations = locations_2d,
  max.edge = c(0.15, 0.3),
  link = "identity"
)

fit_2d <- fit_fun_2d(x_2d, s_2d)
op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 5))

z_truth <- matrix(truth_2d, nrow = nx, ncol = ny)
image(
  x = sort(unique(grid$x)),
  y = sort(unique(grid$y)),
  z = z_truth,
  main = "True surface",
  xlab = "x",
  ylab = "y",
  col = hcl.colors(20, "BluYl")
)

z_fit <- matrix(fit_2d$posterior$mean, nrow = nx, ncol = ny)
image(
  x = sort(unique(grid$x)),
  y = sort(unique(grid$y)),
  z = z_fit,
  main = "Posterior mean",
  xlab = "x",
  ylab = "y",
  col = hcl.colors(20, "BluYl")
)


par(op)

Useful Outputs

The Matern fitter returns backend-specific components in addition to the common ebnm-style fields:

names(fit_1d)
#>  [1] "posterior"                     "fitted_g"
#>  [3] "fitted_beta"                   "beta_prec"
#>  [5] "beta_mode"                     "log_likelihood"
#>  [7] "log_likelihood_semantics"      "posterior_sampler"
#>  [9] "posterior_spatial_field"       "backend"
#> [11] "laplace_implementation"        "laplace_curvature"
#> [13] "link"                          "prior_family"
#> [15] "pc_penalty"                    "log_likelihood_pc_prior_theta"
#> [17] "laplace_diagnostics"           "matern_objective_context"
#> [19] "data"                          "mesh"
#> [21] "g_init"
names(fit_pc)
#>  [1] "posterior"                     "fitted_g"
#>  [3] "fitted_beta"                   "beta_prec"
#>  [5] "beta_mode"                     "log_likelihood"
#>  [7] "log_likelihood_semantics"      "posterior_sampler"
#>  [9] "posterior_spatial_field"       "backend"
#> [11] "link"                          "prior_family"
#> [13] "pc_penalty"                    "log_likelihood_pc_prior_theta"
#> [15] "matern_objective_context"      "data"
#> [17] "mesh"                          "g_init"
class(fit_1d$mesh)
#> [1] "fm_mesh_1d"   "inla.mesh.1d"
class(fit_1d$inla_result)
#> [1] "NULL"
class(fit_pc$inla_result)
#> [1] "NULL"
head(fit_1d$posterior_spatial_field)
#>   ID      mean         sd 0.025quant  0.5quant 0.975quant      mode kld
#> 1  1 0.1752097 0.05937090 0.05884491 0.1752097  0.2915746 0.1752097   0
#> 2  2 0.1825030 0.05581092 0.07311564 0.1825030  0.2918904 0.1825030   0
#> 3  3 0.2041332 0.05073566 0.10469310 0.2041332  0.3035732 0.2041332   0
#> 4  4 0.2402230 0.04772569 0.14668234 0.2402230  0.3337636 0.2402230   0
#> 5  5 0.2889557 0.04702712 0.19678424 0.2889557  0.3811272 0.2889557   0
#> 6  6 0.3472612 0.04690952 0.25532026 0.3472612  0.4392022 0.3472612   0
#>           var
#> 1 0.003524904
#> 2 0.003114859
#> 3 0.002574107
#> 4 0.002277742
#> 5 0.002211550
#> 6 0.002200503

The mesh is still useful for diagnostics and visualization. The posterior_spatial_field component contains a latent-field summary for the mesh nodes.

  • In exact mode, inla_result is NULL, posterior_sampler is available, and the main log_likelihood is the exact EB marginal likelihood.
  • In pc.penalty mode, inla_result stores the INLA fit object, the main log_likelihood is the package exact objective for identity-link fits or the package Laplace objective for log-link fits, both evaluated at the INLA mode. posterior_sampler is currently a compatibility stub that returns NA.
